1998-07-01.title: Hong Kong Surprise: Politics Steady but Economy Falters
1998-07-01.abstract: Hong Kong is marking first anniversary of its return to China with flurry of events on July 1, but many people feel little reason for celebrating with city feeling effects of Asian economic crisis; Britain's exit from Hong Kong has little to do with economic downturn of its former colony, which has been ravaged by rising unemployment, 20 percent drop in stock market and 40 percent decline in property market in last six months; Government has scrapped gaudier elements of last year's celebration because they seem ill-suited to hard times; politically, Hong Kong has scarcely changed in year since it became part of Communist empire; Beijing, defying predictions by experts, has left Hong Kong largely untouched; photo (M)

1998-07-01.title: Clinton Tells Of Hopes and Risks On Trade
1998-07-01.abstract: Pres Clinton, in address to American Chamber of Commerce in Shanghai, says corruption, environmental neglect, weak financial regulation and restrictive trade practices threaten China's growth and full entry into global economic system; says he hopes that China will make difficult decisions needed to bring it into World Trade Organization on same terms that govern other members (M)

1998-07-01.title: Chinese Say Clinton Makes Them Feel More Free, but for How Long?
1998-07-01.abstract: Broad mix of Chinese people--from students to factory workers to small entrepreneurs--say that Pres Clinton's frank discussions and willingness of China's leaders to openly respond have left them hopeful that China will move toward more democratic political system, and left them feeling bit freer to speak out; photo (M)

1998-07-01.title: U.N. Team Says Congo Army Killed Hutu
1998-07-01.abstract: Human rights investigators whom Sec Gen Kofi Annan was forced to withdraw from Congo in April accuse Congolese soldiers and their allies in Rwandan Army of killing unarmed Hutu refugees in 1996 and 1997; team's report, incomplete because of difficulties in gathering evidence, does not estimate total number of Hutu killed, but cites individual massacres of up to 500 people; criticizes Government of Pres Laurent Kabila for blocking inspectors' work (M)

1998-07-01.title: Glitches Snarl Malaysia's New Airport
1998-07-01.abstract: SEPANG, Malaysia—  Kuala Lumpur's new airport opened Tuesday with glitches in its central computer causing the baggage and check-in systems to shut, bringing chaos to Southeast Asia's biggest airport.

1998-07-02.title: SUHARTO REBUILDS HIS POLITICAL BASE TO SHIELD FORTUNE
1998-07-02.abstract: Former Pres Suharto of Indonesia is maneuvering to rebuild political power base to shield his family's financial empire from scrutiny by future Governments and help his children keep their top posts in ruling Golkar party; he and his six children are offering to underwrite political campaigns of legislators who vow loyalty to family; Suharto does not seem to be seeking reinstatement as President, but remains chairman of party's patrons board and retains close ties to military; two of his children are on seven-member organizing committee for congress and four are members of highest legislative body (M)

1998-07-02.title: Pakistani Says A-Strike Was Planned On India
1998-07-02.abstract: Dr Iftikhar Khan Chaudhry, who claims to be defector from Pakistan's nuclear arms program, says in New York that Pakistan's top military leadership, fearing imminent Indian nuclear attack, decided on April 25 to launch pre-emptive nuclear strike on New Delhi within 48 hours; says he was dismissed for opposing attack and his wife is being held by internal security forces; seeks asylum in United States; Pakistan says Khan is fraud who made up story to win US citizenship (M)

1998-07-02.title: Revival of a Synagogue Wins First Lady's Praise
1998-07-02.abstract: Rabbi Arthur Schneier sanctifies long neglected Shanghai synagogue in rite timed to coincide with Hillary Rodham Clinton's visit to Shanghai; city has not yet agreed to let restored synagogue be used as place of worship by its 200-plus member expatriate Jewish community; China does not include Judaism among five officially recognized religions; Mrs Clinton lauds restoration; State Sec Madeleine K Albright also attends rite (M)

1998-07-02.title: Parliament Testily Backs Parts of Plan Yeltsin Seeks
1998-07-02.abstract: Russia's lower house of Parliament hastily approves parts of Pres Boris N Yeltsin's latest economic austerity plan after members criticize plan at length; approves 20 percent value-added tax, new flat tax on small businesses, cut in corporate profits tax and repeal of tax on companies forced to sell products at loss (M)

1998-07-02.title: Two States Outline Sanctions on Swiss Banks in Holocaust Case
1998-07-02.abstract: New York and California outline sanctions against Credit Suisse and UBS after talks collapse over how much money Swiss banks and Swiss Government will return to Holocaust survivors and heirs; Treas Matt Fong, defying State Department, says California will not do any more business with banks; New York State Comptroller, H Carl McCall, and New York City Comptroller, Alan G Hevesi, say they will detail series of sanctions that will take effect in 45 to 60 days unless settlement is reached; three officials, and others from Pennsylvania and Delaware, say they will urge 800 other state and local finance officials to use sanctions to prod Switzerland into joining new talks (M)

1998-07-03.title: With All of the Hoopla, U.S. Envoy's Star Rises
1998-07-03.abstract: James R Sasser, United States Ambassador to China, is taking great satisfaction in Pres Clinton's visit to China; new warmth in Chinese-American relations is in part a personal victory for Sasser, 61, who came to China in early 1996 as a very political appointee after loss of his seat as Democratic Senator from Tennessee, and at a time when China and United States were all but hostile; Sasser's Southern style--polite, charming and attentive--was evident hit with Pres Jiang Zemin; photo (M)

1998-07-03.title: World Bank, A Bit Nervous, Resumes Aid To Indonesia
1998-07-03.abstract: World Bank resumes aid to Indonesia for first time since Pres Suharto was forced from office; approves $1 billion loan and predicts that major industrial nations will soon announce another $6 billion in aid; $1 billion loan is part of $4.5 billion contribution from World Bank that was promised earlier this year (M)

1998-07-03.title: African Opportunity
1998-07-03.abstract: R W Apple Jr analysis state that Nigeria's pledge that it will release all political prisoners is most gratifying news from Africa since establishment of multiracial Government in South Africa under Nelson Mandela; notes that if Nigeria could take even a few steps along path toward democracy, it could encourage other African governments to follow (M)

1998-07-03.title: G.O.P. Delivers Unusual Praise On Clinton Trip
1998-07-03.abstract: Many prominent Republicans concede Pres Clinton's trip to China has been political success that could deprive their party of potent election-year issue; say they still have serious questions about wisdom of Clinton's policy approaches toward Beijing (M)

1998-07-03.title: Annan Says Nigeria Plans to Free A Candidate and Other Prisoners
1998-07-03.abstract: UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan says that Nigeria's new military Government pledges to release all remaining political prisoners, among them Moshood K O Abiola, who was jailed after apparently winning 1993 presidential election; announcement follows meeting with country's President, Gen Abdulsalam Abubakar; chronology of major events in Nigeria since 1993; photo of Abiola (L)

1998-07-04.title: In Upbeat Farewell, Clinton Hails Jiang and Sees Democracy for China
1998-07-04.abstract: Excerpts from news conference by Pres Clinton in Hong Kong at end of his nine-day trip to China; photo (M)

1998-07-04.title: 'Manhattan Of Asia' Indifferent To Clinton
1998-07-04.abstract: Pres Clinton winds up his visit to China with one-day stop in Hong Kong; receives tepid response to speech advocating speedier introduction of democracy under Chinese rule; photo (M)

1998-07-04.title: Persian Gulf Journal; Muting Anguish Over '88 Air Disaster
1998-07-04.abstract: Iran holds shipboard memorial ceremony in Persian Gulf at site where Iran Air jetliner was shot down by US naval cruiser on July 3, 1988, killing all 290 aboard; Iranians appear torn between determination to spotlight the tragedy and desire to follow lead of Pres Mohammad Khatami and soften their tone toward US; photo (Persian Gulf Journal) (M)

1998-07-04.title: Dominant Mexican Party Tries Out Critic's Role
1998-07-04.abstract: Mexico's creeping, 15-year transition from one-party rule is charging upcoming gubernatorial elections with tremendous symbolism; PRI party has controlled the presidency and nearly everything else in Mexico for most of seven decades but last year lost control of the Congress and Mexico City's municipal government; gubernatorial race in Chihuahua, one of six states controlled by National Action Party (PAN), is drawing most attention; map; photo (M)

1998-07-04.title: Among Hosts, Much Elation
1998-07-04.abstract: News analysis: Chinese, from top leaders to the man in the street, were elated with nine-day visit by Pres Clinton, who struck many as vibrant and empathetic; Clinton's gentle lecturing is seen as coming from a respectful friend, and Pres Jiang Zemin's startling decision to allow Clinton to speak on live television only added to the sense of excitement; visit has bolstered status of Pres Jiang, as well as other pragmatic officials from Prime Minister Zhu Rogji on down, who believe that nation's progress depends on better ties with US; but unresolved differences are likely to rear up again, including US's growing trade deficit with China, its refusal to consider Chinese admission to World Trade Organization until painful changes are made to open markets, and concerns over human rights (M)

1998-07-05.title: An Underwater Site In Cuba Casts Light On a Lost Culture
1998-07-05.abstract: Ecavations led by David M Pendergast, Canadian archeologists, at underwater site in Punta Alegre, Cuba, shed new light on Taino people, who inhabited Caribbean islands before arrival of Columbus and who disappeared shortly thereafter; he and Cuban specialists have uncovered a nearly complete community building; site may also hold preserved remains of as many as 25 Taino houses; artifacts support speculation that isolation of Punta Alegre allowed its community to survive Spanish colonization longer than most; photos; map (M)

1998-07-05.title: Albright Hugs Wary Tokyo Smarting From Beijing Trip
1998-07-05.abstract: Sec of State Madeleine K Albright stops in Tokyo on her return from China, reassuring the Japanese that US values its ties with them as much as ever and prodding Prime Minister Ryutaro Hashimoto to take further steps to bolster Japanese economy; many Japanese are hurt that Pres Clinton would spend nine days in China without even a brief visit to Japan; Japanese scholars have often noted that US has historically tilted either toward Japan or China but has difficulty being friendly simultaneously with both (M)

1998-07-05.title: Troops Occupy Ulster Town on Eve of Protestant March
1998-07-05.abstract: British soldiers ring the Drumcree neighborhood of Portadown, Northern Ireland, in effort to prevent sectarian violence on July 5, when Orange Order says it will try to march down down a Catholic street to celebrate 1690 Battle of the Boyne; photo (M)

1998-07-05.title: Arab Christians Protest Sale Of Jerusalem Land to Jews
1998-07-05.abstract: Palestinian Orthodox Christians demonstrate in Jerusalem, charging that Greek Orthodox Patriarchate is selling or leasing to Israelis church lands in Nazareth, Jaffa and Jerusalem and is not using the money this earns to benefit the church or its local Arab members; Patriarchate is reputed to be second-largest property owner in Israel, after Israeli Government; the Orthodox, once the dominant Christian denomination in Holy Land, now account for less than a third of Arab Christians; photo (M)

1998-07-05.title: Clinton's Sojourn in China, Viewed From the Cookout
1998-07-05.abstract: Most people in Springfield, Ill, are pleased with Pres Clinton's visit to China; believe China is too big a force to ignore, given growing importance of global economy; some people accuse Clinton of giving a poor defense of rights of political dissenters and using trip to distract from embarrassing reports spilling out of investigations in Washington; photos (M)

1998-07-06.title: A 6-Hour Move, and Landing in Hong Kong Loses a Thrill
1998-07-06.abstract: Hong Kong closes old Kai Tak International Airport, switching new flights to new $20 billion Hong Kong International Airport built on reclaimed land 19 miles away; move marks end of 73-year-old airport that won hearts of some for its in-town location and stopped those of others with its spectacular, roof-skimming approaches; photo; map (M)

1998-07-06.title: After a Year, No Letup in Asia's Economic Crisis
1998-07-06.abstract: Year-old Asian economic crisis is growing more intense, instead of abating as many had hoped, and stands as largest single threat to six-year economic boom in US; while effects on most Americans have been remote, factories are beginning to slow, exports are beginning to pile up and some members of Congress are calling for swift action; from White House to International Monetary Fund, officials are concerned that effects of crisis will be worse and longer-lasting than first thought; what particularly scares Clinton Administration officials is that unpredictable economic contagion is no longer limited to Pacific Rim (M)

1998-07-06.title: Northern Ireland Relatively Calm as Protestant Marchers Are Kept From Catholic Area
1998-07-06.abstract: British Government, in move to prevent clashes between Catholic minority and Protestant majority, deploys hundreds of combat-ready troops and Northern Ireland policemen to prevent several thousand Protestants from marching--as Protestants have since 1807--through small Roman Catholic enclave of Portadown; members of Orange Order halt march at police barricade, dispersing without violence; photo; map (M)

1998-07-06.title: Peru Reporters Say Officials Direct Harassment Effort
1998-07-06.abstract: Independent Peruvian journalists--who have long been subject to arrests, bombings, wiretapping and other overt forms of censorship--find themselves in middle of new campaign ostensibly by competing news organizations that has galvanized local news media to hold unusual unity protests; daily articles in mainstream press questioning character and patriotism of leading reporter, Angel Paez, and nine others who have also investigated corruption and human rights abuses have inspired solidarity among international press groups and even drawn Clinton Administration into fray in support of reporters; appears to have been orchestrated by Government security officials; Pres Alberto K Fujimori promises investigation; acknowledges Government officials may be involved, but says it is without his authorization; several journalists say they believe Fujimori; say anti-press campaign illuminates fissures between him and senior military and intelligence officials (M)

1998-07-06.title: Marne-la-Vallee Journal; Mouse No More, It's Militant Mickey
1998-07-06.abstract: French employees who play Mickey Mouse and Pluto and other Disney characters at Disneyland Paris have been on picket lines for nearly two weeks, striking for better pay; picketing outside main gate in Marne-la-Vallee is carried out so as not to disrupt park's cheerful operations; are taking place at time when Euro Disney expects to report profit for third consecutive summer, after starting heavily in red in 1992; map; photo (M)

1998-07-07.title: Cows Cloned In First Case Like Dolly's, Japanese Say
1998-07-07.abstract: Japanese scientists report creating two calves that were clones of adult cows; used much same method as Dr Ian Wilmut of Roslin Institute in Scotland in cloning of sheep named Dolly (M)

1998-07-07.title: Gloom in German East May End Kohl's Streak
1998-07-07.abstract: Helmut Kohl is fighting uphill battle for fifth term as German Chancellor; stubbornly high unemployment in former East Germany and nationwide malaise confront Kohl and Christian Democrats with cruel irony that German unification, the biggest single triumph of his 16 years in office, could could deprive his coalition of majority in Parliament; economic tribulations in eastern Germany are of particular significance since politically volatile region has swing voters who can make or break narrow margins of success; election on September 27 could fundamentally redraw Germany's political landscape and determine future of Europe, just as continent confronts upheavals of globalization and its own striving for economic unity; map; charts; photos (M)

1998-07-07.title: Clinton Ends Delay In Setting Russia Trip
1998-07-07.abstract: President Clinton has agreed to travel to Russia in early September to meet with President Boris N. Yeltsin, although the Russian legislature has not fulfilled a request to ratify an important arms control treaty, the White House announced.

1998-07-07.title: No Policy Turn, U.S. Assures Taiwan Again
1998-07-07.abstract: Clinton Administration moves to assure Taiwan Government that there has been no change in American policy toward Taiwan, despite Pres Clinton's declaration in China that US would not support Taiwan's independence from mainland; Clinton's comments, which set off alarms in Taiwan, duplicated statements made in past by other officials, but marked first time President articulated policy himself (S)

1998-07-07.title: PRI Retakes Chihuahua in Symbolic Election
1998-07-07.abstract: Mexico's governing Institutional Revolutionary Party wins gubernatorial election in Chihuahua state, which it lost to National Action (PAN) in 1992; PAN, pro-business party, was regarded as opposition's best chance to win presidency in 2000; map; photo of Gov-elect Patricio Martinez Garcia (M)

1998-07-08.title: Scientists Say Pakistani Defector Is Not Credible
1998-07-08.abstract: Scientists who questioned Iftikhar Khan Chaudhry, who claims to be defector from Pakistan's nuclear arms program, say he know nothing about nuclear physics; Khan, seeking asylum in United States, claimed he was present at meeting at which Pakistan's military high command authorized preemptive nuclear attack on New Delhi (M)

1998-07-08.title: Jailed Nigerian Dies Amid Envoys' Visit
1998-07-08.abstract: Moshood K Abiola, Nigeria's most prominent political prisoner, dies of apparent heart attack during visit with American and Nigerian officials in capital of Abuja; US State Department spokesman James P Rubin says there is nothing to suggest that death was from other than natural causes; says Abiola fell ill during meeting with Under Sec Thomas R Pickering and Asst Sec Susan E Rice; Pickering says Abiola initially seemed in good spirits but that his condition quickly deteriorated; Pres Clinton praises him as distinguished citizen and patriot of Nigeria; UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan, who met with Abiola week ago, calls on Nigerian Government to unconditionally release all remaining political prisoners and to define credible process for transition to civilian rule; Abiola's two daughters voice suspicions about death; say Government is culpable since it denied him medical treatment during confinement; Nigerian Government promises full, prompt autopsy in cooperation with Abiola's personal physicians; some political implications of his passing discussed; photos (M)

1998-07-08.title: Pakistan Says India Uses False Threats to Justify Nuclear Arms
1998-07-08.abstract: Pakistani Foreign Sec Shamshad Ahmed holds India uses fictional threat from China and unverified charges of Pakistani terrorist infiltration into Kashmir to justify its nuclear arms program and revive threats against Pakistan, speech to Asia Society, New York City; urges neutral international observers be stationed on both sides to verify charges; rejects suggestion that Kashmir cease-fire line be made border to ease tensions (M)

1998-07-08.title: Volkswagen, in Shift, Will Set Up Fund for Slave Workers
1998-07-08.abstract: Volkswagen AG, in abrupt shift, agrees to set up fund for workers forced into slave labor during World War II; has insisted in past that Government should compensate victims because company acted on Government orders; faces suit by Hungarian Jews now living in Israel who were forced to work as teen-agers; Klaus von Muenchhausen, who leads compensation drive, sees decision boosting efforts to win payments from other German wartime companies; says Lower Saxony government, which owns 20% of Volkswagen, apparently pressed company to act to avoid unpopular court battle (M)

1998-07-08.title: Attend Mass On Sundays, Pope Reminds The Wayward
1998-07-08.abstract: Pope John Paul II reminds Catholics of their obligation to attend Sunday Mass, apostolic letter; reaffirms church stand that missing Mass is sin; deplores low attendance at Mass (M)

1998-07-09.title: Tillman Durdin, 91, Reporter In China During World War II
1998-07-09.abstract: Tillman Durdin, longtime foreign correspondent for The New York Times who was one of the first to write about the Japanese atrocities in China that became known as the Rape of Nanking, dies at age of 91; photo (M)

1998-07-09.title: Japanese Premier Promises Tax Cut for Long Term, Too
1998-07-09.abstract: Prime Min Ryutaro Hashimoto promises long-term tax cuts beginning next year, but he gives no details about how much move might fatten pocketbooks or lift sagging Japanese economy; Japan is under enormous pressure from United States and other countries to make permanent tax cuts to stimulate economy and reduce risk of global downturn; Hashimoto has been reluctant to lower minimum level of taxable income, because any tax cuts would have to be financed somehow by Government that has just declared fiscal austerity to be its medium-term goal (M)

1998-07-09.title: Not to Debunk the Berlin Airlift, but Have You Heard This?
1998-07-09.abstract: Lothar Schulze was one of hundreds of thousands of Berliners under Soviet occupation when Berlin airlift started in response to Moscow's bloclade of Western sectors of city in June 1948; Schulze says blockade was more porous than history has suggested; says guards enforcing separation were often bribed to look the other way when goods were exchanged between two sectors of Berlin (M)

1998-07-09.title: Demre Journal; Will the World Buy This? Santa Claus Lived Here
1998-07-09.abstract: Demre, on Turkey's Mediterranean coast, has been center for pagans, early Christians and modern Eastern Orthodox denominations; now it is attracting camera-toting pilgrims who have heard stories of ancient miracles, grave robbers, dervishes and disappearing bones; local holy man named Nicholas, who died in 323, is said to have been prototype of Santa Claus; photos; map (Demre Journal) (M)

1998-07-09.title: Nigerian Pleads for Calm, Vowing Democracy and Autopsy for Foe
1998-07-09.abstract: Nigeria's military ruler, Gen Abdulsalam Abubakar, tries to calm angry nation following sudden death of imprisoned opposition leader, Moshood K O Abiola; Abubakar agrees to allow outside experts to take part in autopsy, and makes vague promises about moving toward democracy; his gesture appears unlikely to have any immediate impact because power lies with military men on Provisional Ruling Council; country seems poised on knife-edge; riots that followed Abiola's death underscore explosive resonance of his name; situation is expected to remain highly volatile at least until results of autopsy are known; photos (M)

1998-07-10.title: THE BABY BUST: A special report.; Population Implosion Worries a Graying Europe
1998-07-10.abstract: The Baby Bust, special report on rapid, sustained decline in birth rates in many countries around world; millions of women throughout developed world are choosing to have fewer children, driven largely by prosperity and freedom; women stay in school longer, put more emphasis on work and marry later; never before--except at times of plague, war and deep economic depression--have birth rates fallen so low, for so long; low birth rates, once regarded as industrial world's cherished goal, are now cause for alarm; with life expectancy rising at same time that fertility drops, most developed countries may soon find themselves with lopsided societies that will be nearly impossible to sustain: large number of elderly and not enough young people working to support them; change will affect every program that requires public funds; nowhere in Europe are people having enough children to replace themselves when they die; large pool of new immigrants to US help keep fertility rate higher than in any other prosperous country, but figure is still below 2.1 children per woman--magic number needed to keep population from starting to shrink; photos; maps (L)

1998-07-10.title: U.S. Specifies Terms for War Crimes Court
1998-07-10.abstract: United States sets out new conditions for taking part in international criminal court, calling for sharp limits on its scope and authority; Washington has long made clear its opposition to independent tribunal with broad powers to investigate war crimes suspects across globe; US now says it could agree to court only if its automatic jurisdiction were limited to genocide, which it views as worst outrage; Washington's primary objection is that American soldiers who unwittingly kill civilians during peacekeeping operation might be vulnerable to politically motivated charges (M)

1998-07-10.title: Senators Back Sale of Wheat To Pakistanis
1998-07-10.abstract: Senate unanimously agrees to exempt food exports from sanctions that United States imposed on India and Pakistan after two countries conducted nuclear tests in May; vote, in response to pressure from American wheat farmers, lifts largest and most painful economic penalty Washington inflicted and leaves almost no meaningful sanctions in place to punish two countries for tests; India and Pakistan are small markets for American non-farm goods; Senators express concern about threat of new arms race, but they have important farming constituents to consider, and wheat prices have plunged to seven-year low because of excess supply; bipartisan bill was rushed to floor in advance of Pakistan's planned purchase of 350,000 metric tons of wheat in next week (M)

1998-07-10.title: Hadera Journal; Jewish Family Heirloom: 15 Square Miles of Death
1998-07-10.abstract: Zypora Frank, 62-year-old Israeli teacher, finds out that her family owned part of land on which Auschwitz death camp stood, where most of her mother's family perished; discovery of deed to land ends her quest to learn exactly what the properties were that her parents told her about 10 years ago; Frank has only vague childhood memories of fleeing her homeland and taking tortuous route that ended in Israel; photo (M)

1998-07-10.title: Prime Minister Accused of Favoring Lobbyists : Blair Beset in Scandal Over Money for Access
1998-07-10.abstract: LONDON—  Of all the forces that swept Tony Blair's Labour Party into government last year, few were as powerful as the string of bribery scandals involving Conservative politicians that allowed Mr. Blair to campaign against "Tory sleaze."

1998-07-11.title: Bernard Haring, 85, Is Dead; Challenged Catholic Morality
1998-07-11.abstract: Rev Bernard Haring, Roman Catholic scholar who influenced Vatican II by stressing moral theology of Christian love, dies at age 85; photo (M)

1998-07-11.title: Saudi Prince Insists an Attack That Killed 19 Americans Is Still Being Investigated
1998-07-11.abstract: Saudi Prince Abdul Aziz bin Fahd insists probe of 1996 terrorist bombing that killed 19 American airmen in Dhahran, Saudi Arabia, continues, news conference, Washington, after meeting with Pres Clinton; says Saudi officials cooperate with American counterparts; contradicts stand of American officials, who say probe broke down because of disputes with Saudis (M)

1998-07-11.title: Hong Kong Journal; Latest Letdown: Airport's Nosedive
1998-07-11.abstract: Hong Kong's lavish new airport is afflicted by bizarre string of mishaps that mar its opening week; Hong Kong residents are embarrassed and outraged over breakdowns; Hong Kong chief executive Tung Chee-hwa sets probe; photo (M)d

1998-07-11.title: U.S. Aides Say Nigeria Leader Might Have Been Poisoned
1998-07-11.abstract: Some United States intelligence analysts say Nigerian dictator Gen Sani Abacha may have been poisoned by enemies in his authoritarian military circle; other officials say they lack firm evidence; rumors that Abacha was poisoned have been circulating in Nigeria for weeks (M)

1998-07-11.title: YELTSIN IN APPEAL DIRECTLY TO WEST FOR BILLIONS IN AID
1998-07-11.abstract: Russian Pres Boris Yeltsin, in extraordinary series of appeals, phones Pres Clinton, French Pres Jacques Chirac, British Prime Min Tony Blair, German Chancellor Helmut Kohl and International Monetary Fund director Michel Camdessus seeking $20 billion or more in Western aid to defend Russia's currency; hopes $10 billion will come from IMF, twice what fund has spoken of providing; total aid bid, which is $5 billion to $10 billion larger than Russia previously said it needed, reflects Government's fears about rapidly deteriorating economic situation, which poses threat to political stability; Clinton expresses support for Yeltsin Government, which is locked in difficult talks with IMF; Prime Min Sergei Kiriyenko tells Parliament that situation has worsened on financial markets; Yeltsin warns 'extremists' against exploiting crisis to oust him, speech to senior military officers (M)

1998-07-12.title: A Cuban Exile Details the 'Horrendous Matter' of a Bombing Campaign
1998-07-12.abstract: Antonio Jorge Alvarez, Cuban-American businessman, claims that he tried to alert Federal Bureau of Investigation to bombing campaign in Cuba being conducted by Luis Posada Carriles, but that FBI appeared uninterested and never followed up on his call; says Venezuelan authorities, who also received his warnings, were concerned, especially about upcoming conference of Latin American heads of state, including Fidel Castro, to be held in Venezuela; Alvarez, who is bitter about his experience, says he believes Posada is in cahoots with FBI; photos (M)

1998-07-12.title: Ulster Foes in Last-Ditch Effort to Avoid a Parade Clash
1998-07-12.abstract: Protestant and Roman Catholic officials in Northern Ireland refuse to meet one another face to face, but have several hours of contact through intermediaries, trying to prevent increasing violence over Protestant Orange Order's insistence that it be allowed to march through resentful Catholic neighborhood in Portadown; only slight progess is reported before session is adjourned, heightening risk of more violence; impasse over march is matter of face-saving on both sides; neither wants to be vulnerable to charges that it flinched or backed down (M)

1998-07-12.title: Indonesian Leader Makes Important Gain in Party Election
1998-07-12.abstract: Indonesia's new President, B J Habibie, consolidates his hold over country's politics after one of his close advisers, Akbar Tanjung, wins hotly contested vote to lead country's dominant political party; Habibie's control over party greatly improves his ability to set political agenda and remain in office at least until end of next year, when he has scheduled parliamentary vote for new President; photo (M)

1998-07-12.title: How a Swiss Bank Gold Deal Eluded a U.S. Mediator
1998-07-12.abstract: Switzerland's hard-line position is blamed for failed negotiations between country's three major commercial banks and Holocaust survivors; Stuart E Eizenstat, Under Secretary of State for Economic Affairs, reportedly placed urgent call to Pres Flavio Cotti of Switzerland, asking Cotti to reverse long-held position and have Swiss Government contribute to 'global settlement' with Jewish groups and Holocaust victims and their heirs; Eizenstat reminded Cotti that Swiss National Bank, which is Government-owned, received more than 85 percent of gold deposited by Nazi Germany; Cotti refused even to bring issue in front of legislature, saying it would only further inflame public opinion (M)

1998-07-12.title: U.S. Hoping For a Deal On Pullback By Israelis
1998-07-12.abstract: Israeli and American officials expect conclusion to negotiations on Israeli pullback from West Bank by end of July; refuse to predict whether conclusion will be successful; say Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright has had at least seven conversations in last week with Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu, some of them heated; she spoke to Palestinian leader, Yasir Arafat, as well; Americans are resisting Netanyahu's request to send special Middle East envoy, Dennis B Ross, back to region, until deal is done; American plan calls for Israeli withdrawal from further 13 percent of West Bank over 12 weeks, in return for specific and measurable Palestinian actions against terrorism and immediate start to negotiations on final settlement with Palestinians (M)

1998-07-13.title: Russia Reaches Pact For Billions in Loans
1998-07-13.abstract: The Russian Government said it had reached agreement with the International Monetary Fund on $11 billion in new loans to help stabilize its financial markets.

1998-07-13.title: Burying Dreams: Abiola's Death Stalls Movement
1998-07-13.abstract: Roger Cohen analysis contends that death of Nigerian opposition leader Moshood K O Abiola has derailed his supporters hopes for democracy; photo (M)

1998-07-13.title: Dead Diver From North Puts Seoul on Alert
1998-07-13.abstract: South Korean military commanders put troops on country's eastern coast on alert and declare curfew in area after finding body of diver who they say was North Korean commando; map (M)

1998-07-13.title: JAPANESE PREMIER RESIGNS AS VOTERS REBUKE HIS PARTY
1998-07-13.abstract: Prime Minister Ryutaro Hashimoto resigns after voters rebuke Japan's governing party in parliamentary elections; several senior officials in Liberal Democratic Party are jockeying to succeed Hashimoto, and decision is expected soon, to reassure unsettled financial markets throughout Asia; transition is sure to create new uncertainties about Japan's political and economic course; since July 12 elections were for upper house of Parliament, not more important lower house, they did not directly affect Hashimoto's post as Prime Minister or his party's lock on power; Hashimoto billed vote as referendum on his leadership, and party won only 44 of 126 seats up for election; photos (M)

1998-07-13.title: Violence Spreads in Zulu Area as New Party Gathers Force
1998-07-13.abstract: New battle seems to be emerging between South Africa's newest political party, United Democratic Movement, and Pres Nelson Mandela's party, African National Congress; political violence in KwaZulu/Natal Province has left 23 people dead in last week; movement is headed in area by local strongman, Sifiso Nkabinde; maps (M)

1998-07-14.title: I.M.F. Says It Will Borrow From Members
1998-07-14.abstract: IMF, its financial strength depleted by Asian crisis, will borrow from richest members to pay for bailout of Russia; new loan package will largely tap backup credit line from US and other industrial countries (S)

1998-07-14.title: Berlusconi Gets 2d Conviction in Week
1998-07-14.abstract: ROME—  Silvio Berlusconi, the media tycoon and Italian opposition leader, was convicted Monday of using an offshore company to channel $12 million in bribes to former Prime Minister Bettino Craxi and his defunct Socialist Party.

1998-07-14.title: Calm Ulster Marches After Violent Week
1998-07-14.abstract: Tens of thousands of Protestants paraded peacefully in Northern Ireland. The marches followed a week of Protestant upheaval over the issue of patriotic marches in Roman Catholic neighborhoods and the death of three young Catholic brothers in an arson attack.

1998-07-14.title: Relief Is Felt in Tokyo That the Message Has Finally Gotten Across
1998-07-14.abstract: Japanese voters sigh in relief that their political message may have finally gotten through to top leadership; welcome Prime Min Ryutaro Hashimoto's resignation; 'real scolding' for Liberal Democratic party may cause even more delay on economic reform to get country out of recession, but trade-off may be worth it on emotional level; photo (S)

1998-07-14.title: Russia and Lenders Seal Accord On $17 Billion in New Support
1998-07-14.abstract: Russian Government and international lenders conclude agreement aimed at easing Russia's financial crisis and restoring confidence in its economy; agreement is to provide $17.1 billion in additional loans over next two, about $12.6 billion this year and $4.5 billion in 1999; most of new money comes from IMF under pressure from Clinton Administration; US is lending fund $2.1 billion, with other money from Japan, Germany, France and Saudi Arabia; Anatoly Chubais says negotiations were most difficult ever with IMF; photo; table; Russia still must take important steps to get funds, including lowering tax rates to stimulate investments and new budgeting procedures; IMF negotiator John Odling-Smee sees strengthening of economic program that will avert devaluation (M)

1998-07-15.title: At Boys' Funeral in Ulster: Grim Sorrow, a Glint of Hope
1998-07-15.abstract: Funeral for 11-year-old Richard Quinn and brothers, Mark, 10, and Jason, 9, is held in Rasharkin, Northern Ireland; brothers were burned to death in beds in firebombing of row house in Ballymoney on July 12; they were among the few Catholics in predominantly Protestant housing project; leaders of Protestant Orange Order continue their confrontation with government security forces at Drumcree, outside Portadown, despite calls from Protestant clerics and politicians to withdraw out of respect for the boys; photos; maps (M)

1998-07-15.title: Cambodian Government Tries to Bully Voters, U.N. Is Told
1998-07-15.abstract: United Nations officials following preparations for July 26 elections in Cambodia say country's electoral institutions are heavily weighted in favor of Cambodian People's Party of Prime Min Hun Sen, who controls all broadcasting and has barred coverage of opposition campaigns; say voter intimidation has been widespread and Hun's supporters still operate in climate of impunity that has led to dozens of killings and disappearances (M)

1998-07-15.title: Phnom Penh Journal; When He Writes a Song, Cambodia Better Listen
1998-07-15.abstract: Hun Sen, Second Prime Minister of Cambodia, is also one of country's most prolific songwriters; his words are put to popular melodies, recorded onto cassettes and played every evening on Government-controlled radio stations; their appeal to Cambodians is hard to judge; photo (M)

1998-07-15.title: North Korea Said to Block Inspection of Nuclear Sites
1998-07-15.abstract: General Accounting Office reports that North Korea is refusing to allow international inspectors full access to its nuclear sites, raising concern that it will be impossible to determine whether North has hidden away enough plutonium to build nuclear weapons; says there are significant gaps in inspection program imposed by 1994 nuclear freeze agreement signed with US (M)

1998-07-15.title: Pomp and Soccer, and Some Politics, at a Bastille Day Party
1998-07-15.abstract: Pres Jacques Chirac, in a Bastille Day appeal, asks his French compatriots not to lose upbeat spirit born of World Cup soccer championship; says victory shows solidarity and cohesion; photo (M)

1998-07-16.title: World News Briefs; Iranian Cleric Nominated For Ousted Minister's Job
1998-07-16.abstract: Pres Mohammad Khatami of Iran nominates Vice Pres Abdol Vahed Moussavi-Lari as Interior Minister (S)

1998-07-16.title: G.O.P., IN SWITCH, BACKS MORE FUNDS TO SHORE UP I.M.F.
1998-07-16.abstract: House Appropriations subcommittee approves $3.5 billion for International Monetary Fund as Republicans grow worried that economic crises overseas may spread to US; House majority leader, Dick Armey, harsh foe of IMF, virtually concedes fight; $3.5 billion is far below $17.9 billion requested by Clinton Administration, but members of panel signal that they recognize urgency of agency's need for more financing; Asian financial crisis threatens US agricultural exports, intensifying pressure on Republicans to drop objections to fund (M)

1998-07-16.title: World Bank Suspends 3 Employees for Suspected Kickbacks
1998-07-16.abstract: World Bank is investigating possible kickbacks and other wrongdoing by at least three employees (S)

1998-07-16.title: A Uganda Tribe Fights Genital Cutting
1998-07-16.abstract: G W Cheborian, leader of Sabiny clan in Kapchorwa, Uganda, discusses tribe's campaign to abolish female genital mutilation and replace it with symbolic ritual declaring girl a woman without maiming her for life; map (M)

1998-07-16.title: Rape Cases Shake Up The Army In Canada
1998-07-16.abstract: Many Canadians are upset over reports of sexual assaults at gunpoint and widespread acceptance of crude behavior toward women in military; although many soldiers say reports about rape in military are unfair and inaccurate, others say they are not surprised that such abuses may have occurred because of officers' pervasive indifference to such concerns (M)

1998-07-17.title: Morse Code Sinks Without an SOS
1998-07-17.abstract: PARIS—  As the inventor Samuel Morse might have said:

1998-07-17.title: China Urged to Free 5 Political Activists
1998-07-17.abstract: Human rights group based in Hong Kong says that 100 Chinese dissidents have called on Chinese leaders to free five activists who were detained after trying to register an opposition political party (S)

1998-07-17.title: Cuban Exile Group's Leaders Plan a Lawsuit Against The Times
1998-07-17.abstract: Leaders of a Miami-based Cuban exile group, Cuban American National Foundation, announce that they intend to file lawsuit against The New York Times, contending that newspaper published 'outrageous and malicious' allegations linking group's founder, Jorge Mas Canosa, to bombing campaign in Cuba last year aimed at toppling Fidel Castro; in articles, militant Cuban exile named Luis Posada Carilles is quoted as claiming responsibility for organizing wave of bombings last year at Cuban hotels, restaurants and discotheques that killed an Italian tourist and alarmed Cuban Govt; Posada also claimed that he had received financial support from leaders of foundation, including Jorge Mas Canosa; at news conf, Jorge Mas Santos, Mas's son and group's director, adamantly denies that his father or foundation had supported any of Posada's efforts; Mas Santos demands The Times publish retraction; in statement, The Times 'reiterates its complete support and confidence' in two-part series, which was published on July 12 and 13 (M)

1998-07-17.title: Opium Surge Threatens U.N. Aid to Afghans, Official Warns
1998-07-17.abstract: Top United Nations official, Pino Arlacchi, warns that an anti-narcotics program begun in Afghanistan late last year is in danger of being dismantled, citing reports that opium poppy production may be increasing in areas under control of Islamic Taliban movement; Arlacchi,exec dir of UN Drug Control Program, also says that he is hearing for first time of opening of refineries to produce morphine, if not heroin, near heart of Taliban's religious center of Kandahar; both increased production and refining of opium would violate accord under which United Nations would provide development aid in return for gradual reduction in poppy production; Noorullah Zadran, Taliban's representative-designate to United Nations, asserts that Taliban strongly opposes narcotics but that drug-control program had demanded too much of Taliban too soon; map (M)

1998-07-17.title: C.I.A. Says It Used Nicaraguan Rebels Accused of Drug Tie
1998-07-17.abstract: Classified study by Central Intelligence Agency says that CIA continued to work with about two dozen Nicaraguan rebels and their supporters during 1980's despite allegations that they were trafficking in drugs; new study finds that agency's decision to keep those paid agents, or to continue dealing with them in some less formal relationship, was made by top officials at headquarters in Langley, Va, in midst of war waged by CIA-backed contras against Nicaragua's leftist Sandinista Govt (M)

1998-07-18.title: As Cambodia Nears Election, Leader Has Most of the Cards
1998-07-18.abstract: Cambodian intellectuals, human rights monitors and diplomats say Hun Sen's ruling party has so intimidated electorate and so thoroughly controls election levers that no opposition group stands fair chance of dislodging him in July 26 election; say since violent coup one year ago that gave Hun Sen complete control of Government, over 100 people have been killed in campaign to consolidate ruling party's power; for all of Hun Sen's advantages, outcome of election is far from certain; some 39 parties are vying for votes, and no single party is expected to win two-thirds majority in 122-seat Parliament that is required to form Government; Parliament will then select Prime Minister, who is expected to be one of three nationally known politicians now leading field: Hun Sen, Prince Norodom Ranariddh and Sam Rainsy; map; Hun Sen photo (M)

1998-07-18.title: Ex-Premier Of France To Be Tried In Blood Case
1998-07-18.abstract: Former Prime Min Laurent Fabius, now Speaker of French Parliament, is ordered to stand trial with former ministers Georgina Dufoix and Edmond Herve on charges of manslaughter in deaths of hundreds of patients who received transfusions in 1985 from Government blood stocks contaminated with HIV (M)

1998-07-18.title: Peronists Wrangle Over 3d Term for Argentine President
1998-07-18.abstract: Peronist party splits into two angry camps during convention intended to ease way for Argentine Pres Carlos Saul Menem to seek third term next year, step that is barred under Constitution; nearly 500 party delegates vote unanimously for resolution supporting Menem's re-election bid; Vice Pres Carlos Ruckauf and Buenos Aires Province Gov Eduardo Duhalde, Menem's principal opponent for party nomination, refuse to attend carefully orchestrated meeting, and scores of delegates follow their lead (M)

1998-07-18.title: Parliament In Russia Fails To Pass Bills Tied to Loans
1998-07-18.abstract: Russian Government effort to line up preconditions for $17 billion in new loans falls short when Communist-dominated Parliament fails to adopt series of measures required by International Monetary Fund (M)

1998-07-18.title: U.S. Dissents, but Accord Is Reached on War-Crime Court
1998-07-18.abstract: More than 100 countries, but not United States, reach accord on the fundamentals for an interntional court to prosecute war crimes; delegates to United Nations conference say that without United States backing, the court's authority will be weakened (M)

1998-07-19.title: At Least 600 Drown In Papua New Guinea
1998-07-19.abstract: A 23-foot-high wave crashed into the northern coast of Papua New Guinea, washing away villages built on beaches, killing nearly 600, most of them old people and schoolchildren, and leaving hundreds of others missing, disaster officials said.     Thousands were without food and shelter after the wave hit the southwest Pacific island nation Friday night, following an earthquake of magnitude 7 about 12 miles offshore, the National Disaster Center said.

1998-07-19.title: Philatelic Diplomacy: Palestinians Join Collectors' List
1998-07-19.abstract: Scott Publishing Co's newest catalogue of worldwide stamps will include stamps issued by Palestinian Authority; says international mail centers in Egypt and Jordan routinely process material bearing Palestinian Authority stamps and and postal officials in other countries have not challenged their use; Scott's move raises Palestinian stamps from level of local labels, known as cinderellas, to collectibles, thus increasing their value to dealers; photo (M)

1998-07-19.title: Zapatistas Get Word, Cryptically, From Chief
1998-07-19.abstract: Cryptic fax messages sent Subcommander Marcos, leader of Zapatista rebels in Mexico's Chiapas state, send commentators off on flights of speculation; appear to dispel rumors that Marcos, a military strategist, poet and counterculture celebrity, is dying of malaria, has been captured by Guatemalan army or has gone to France for World Cup soccer championship; photo (M)

1998-07-19.title: In Ulster, Still Horror, Still Hope
1998-07-19.abstract: Warren Hoge analysis of prospects for political solution to Northern Ireland's troubles in view of persistent antagonism between Protestants and Catholics; says communities across the province continue to flaunt their religious exclusivity and people either identify themselves as Irish (Catholic) or British (Protestant); sees some evidence that religiously polarized politics of province may be breaking down (M)

1998-07-19.title: Anger Is Shaking a Dead Nigerian's Town to Its Foundations
1998-07-19.abstract: Abeokuta, Nigeria, hometown of the opposition leader, Moshood K O Abiola, is devastated by rioting sparked by his death on July 7; rioting reflects anger of ardent reformers against the annulment of 1993 presidential election, the anger of the myriad poor against wealthy citizens and the anger of the area's dominant Yoruba tribe against the Hausa and Fulani tribes in northern Nigeria, who have tended to dominate the country's successive military governments; photo; map (M)

1998-07-20.title: Rebels Claim First Capture Of a City In Kosovo
1998-07-20.abstract: Ethnic Albanian separatists, in possibly start of significant new phase of fighting in Serbian province of Kosovo, reportedly capture Orahovac, their first city and say they will use newly acquired weapons to keep it; Serbian forces launch counterattack, but separatist forces seem confident, keeping up heavy firing against remaining Government positions in city; map; photo (M)

1998-07-20.title: Europe, Bucking Trend in U.S., Blocks Genetically Altered Food
1998-07-20.abstract: European consumers are in open revolt against genetically engineered food, attesting to cultural and philosophical differences between them and pragmatic, risk-ready Americans, who have largely accepted genetic technology that focuses on food; more than 30 million acres of commercial farmland worldwide were planted with genetically modified seeds in 1997, or 10 times more than year before, but none was in 16 countries of European Union; European conservatism attributed to strong environmental movement rooted in 19th-century philosophy that nature is as wise as man, fear of drastic change and unusually large number of small farms still run by families who are reluctant to end practices that have been honed over centuries; photos (M)

1998-07-20.title: Malaysian Newspaper Shake-Ups Prompt Anxiety About Press Crackdown : Mahathir Attacks 'Negative' Reporting
1998-07-20.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  Following shake-ups at Malaysia's two highest-circulation newspapers, Prime Minister Mahathir bin Mohamad criticized the local media over the weekend for publishing "negative" and "baseless" reports.

1998-07-20.title: Nigerian Slum's Filth Is a World Away From Capital's Glitter
1998-07-20.abstract: Religion is booming in desperate Nigeria, plundered by successive military governments and where divisions between wealthy minority and poor masses are particularly raw; relations have been worsened by scale of country's wealth, $10 billion of which is earned annually from oil, and scale of military misrule that has directed use of resources; widely held perception that northern Muslims, particularly Hausa and Fulani, have arrogated rule and its immense fruits to themselves has injected venom of ethnic resentments; since recent death in detention of Moshood K Abiola, opposition leader from southern Yourba people whose apparent victory in 1993 election was annulled by northern general, anger and suspicion seem to have built to pitch that Gen Abdulsalam Abubakar will be hard presed to allay (M)

1998-07-20.title: Shoppers Unaware of Gene Changes
1998-07-20.abstract: Most American consumers are unaware of amount of genetically engineered food that is being marketed because US, unlike many other countries, does not require labeling of gene-modified food (M)

1998-07-21.title: A 72-Year-Old Young Turk in Japan's Premier Race
1998-07-21.abstract: 72-year-old Seirou Kajiyama, one of three candidates vying to become Prime Minister of Japan, moves to burnish image in West as reformer and to influence choice by leaders of governing party; holds rare news conference, at which he fielded questions about policy proposals, addressed issue of his age and otherwise explained himself (M)

1998-07-21.title: Old Fears Battle New Optimism in East Timor
1998-07-21.abstract: Unsettling mix of terror and hope pervades Dili, capital of disputed territory of East Timor, where solution to two decades of separatist warfare and military brutality suddenly seems possible; terror, born of decades of mutual mistrust, comes from new threats of violence that in recent days have caused thousands to flee territory, which was siezed by Indonesia in 1975; hope comes from peace talks in Jakarta between Indonesian Pres B J Habibie, UN envoy, Roman Catholic bishop and jailed separatist leader; four seem to be moving quickly toward framework that could include period of dialogue followed by some form of autonomy and referendum that could lead to independence; photo; maps (M)

1998-07-21.title: New Leader Of W.H.O. Gets Big Grant To Hire Experts
1998-07-21.abstract: Rockefeller Foundation will provide $2.5 million fund to new World Health Organization director, Gro Harlem Brundtland, to aid her in recruiting top-level experts in next two years; Brundtland, former Norweigan Prime Minister and physician who studied public health at Harvard, plans to revamp upper-level management (M)

1998-07-21.title: Q&A / Valdus Adamkus of Lithuania : Riding High, President Places His Trust in Future
1998-07-21.abstract: President Valdas Adamkus of Lithuania, 72, has just marked six months in office.

1998-07-21.title: Lockerbie Suspects May Be Tried at World Court
1998-07-21.abstract: US and Britain may allow trial in Hague, under Scottish law, for two Libyans accused of blowing up Pan American World Airways jetliner over Lockerbie, Scotland, in 1988; had previously insisted that suspects, Abdul Basset al-Megrahi and Lamin Khalifah Fhimah, be tried in US or Scotland (S)

1998-07-22.title: A Rare Politician With Passion Appeals to Japanese Youth
1998-07-22.abstract: Japanese Health and Welfare Min Junichiro Koizumi is campaigning to become president of Liberal Democratic Party and nation's next Prime Minister; photo (M)

1998-07-22.title: Mobs Reported to Loot Plantations : Loss of Jobs Pushes Indonesia Into Upheaval
1998-07-22.abstract: CIPURINGIN, Indonesia—  What can you do if you lose your job? Millions of Indonesians have.

1998-07-22.title: It's Official: U.S. to Consider Dutch Trial in Lockerbie Case
1998-07-22.abstract: Clinton Administration says that US and Britain will consider creation of special court in Netherlands to try Abdel Basset al-Mehgrahi and Lamin Khalifah Fhimah, Libyans suspected of planting bomb aboard jumbo jet that exploded over Lockerbie, Scotland, in Dec 1988; announcement represents turnaround for Administration, which has said in past that Libyans would have to be tried in US or Britain (M)

1998-07-22.title: U.N. Aide Quits Iraq Relief Post
1998-07-22.abstract: Denis J Halliday, Irish official who ran United Nations program that lets Iraq sell oil and buy food, has decided to leave post after less than year (S)

1998-07-22.title: Fast-Track Trade Bill: A Political Train Wreck?
1998-07-22.abstract: Senate Finance Committee votes to give Pres Clinton enhanced authority to negotiate international trade agreements, setting up unusual political confrontation with White House; Clinton wants authority, but he does not want to address question in election year; legislation is opposed by vast majority of Democrats in House and by labor unions that are Democrats' biggest donors (M)

1998-07-23.title: Taking a Steppe Back / Mongolians Return to Traditional Nomadism : Model City of the Soviet Era Changes Its Plan
1998-07-23.abstract: DARKHAN, Mongolia—  It took peculiar logic, residents of this city say, to erect high-rise towers in the middle of the Mongolian wilderness.

1998-07-23.title: A Post-Apartheid Agony: AIDS on the March
1998-07-23.abstract: AIDS epidemic in South Africa is now one of fastest growing in world; experts say country missed unique opportunity it had to prevent kind of explosion of AIDS cases now ravaging rest of Africa; South Africa had health system better than any other on continent, and for years it was protected by apartheid's isolationism from major exposure to infection; but country was absorbed by its transition from white supremacist rule in early 1990's, and no one paid much attention to halting unprotected sex; experts say Government's efforts to combat epidemic have been slow and marred by scandal; now, about 12 percent of all adults in South Africa are infected with HIV; rate of infection is highest, at 20 percent, among women in their 20's; for now, virus is most prevalent among South Africa's poor blacks; photos; map; charts (L)

1998-07-23.title: Britain Weighs Dutch Trial in Lockerbie Case
1998-07-23.abstract: Britain joins United States in signaling its willingness to consider Netherlands a site of trial for two Libyan suspects in bombing of Pan Am Flight 103, which killed 270 people decade ago in explosion over Lockerbie, Scotland; until now, Britain and United States have insisted that two be tried either in Scottish or American court (M)

1998-07-23.title: Shake-Up Looms for Bankers In Thailand : FROM BANKS TO UNIONS :ASIA WOES MOUNT
1998-07-23.abstract: BANGKOK—  One year after the Asian economic crisis began in Thailand, all 15 of the country's commercial banks reported first-half losses Wednesday that were even more severe than the most pessimistic forecasts.

1998-07-23.title: Despite Risks, U.S. Wants Cambodians to Vote
1998-07-23.abstract: Clinton Administration says it wants to see balloting in Cambodia carried out on time, despite warnings that elections will be marred by violence and will not be free and fair; Administration is spending $2.3 million to underwrite private groups that will monitor election; hope is that Cambodians will rise above intimidation and violence that has marked election campaign and vote their conscience (M)

1998-07-24.title: By Appealing to Rebels, U.N. Chief Makes Peace With Mexico
1998-07-24.abstract: United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan defuses diplomatic crisis with Mexican Govt during visit to Mexico City by calling on Zapatista rebels to return to negotiating table to resolve smoldering conflict in state of Chiapas; repeatedly praises Pres Ernesto Zedillo's strategy in four-year-old face-off between Govt and ragtag guerrilla army with broad base of support in Indian villages (M)

1998-07-24.title: Outcry Erupts Over Palestinians' Statements on Holocaust
1998-07-24.abstract: American Jewish organizations and Holocaust scholars call on Yasir Arafat to reprimand Palestinian officials and journalists who have recently make statements that appear to distort history of Holocaust or deny its existence; inflammatory remarks about Holocaust in Palestinian press also raise concerns among American negotiators who are struggling to revive stalled Middle East peace effort (M)

1998-07-24.title: Pope Tightens Grip by Rome On Its Bishops
1998-07-24.abstract: Pope John Paul II, seeking to curb independence of bishops, issues apostolic letter that tightens Rome's grip over 108 bishops' conferences around world; under new rules, it would be almost impossible for National Conference of Catholic Bishops in United States, for instance, to issue binding statements on matters of doctrine or of public policy topics, like nuclear weapons or status of women, that diverge from Vatican view; apostolic letter is welcomed by head of National Conference of Catholic Bishops but it causes consternation among many American clergymen and theologians, who worry that it will diminish local leaders' ability to deal directly with problems endemic to their own parishes (M)

1998-07-24.title: Israel Cites Its Warnings on Iran but Sees No Missile Peril Now
1998-07-24.abstract: Israel treats Iran's test of medium-range missile as confirmation of its insistent warnings over many months about Iran's efforts to develop long-distance strategic threat (M)

1998-07-24.title: World News Briefs; Russian Tax Problem For Johnson & Johnson
1998-07-24.abstract: Russian tax authorities say that subsidiary of Johnson & Johnson, world's fifth-largest drug maker, owes them almost $19 million in back taxes and that they have begun criminal cases against two of company's managers (S)

1998-07-25.title: In Bosnian Town Where Thousands Died, Ethnic Hate Overwhelms Small Kindnesses
1998-07-25.abstract: Group of 66 men and women are first Bosnian Muslim civilians to peacefully re-enter Srebrenica, nearly three years after ambushes and massacres by Bosnian Serb soldiers left 7,300 Bosnian Muslims presumed dead; no violence erupts during their brief visit, and there are even brief conversations between once-bitter enemies; American officials hail mourners' visit as example of how aggressive Western policies over past year are producing results, even in town with some of country's bitterest ethnic hatreds; critics say military force required to let Muslims visit reflects painstaking progress of mission; there is scant evidence that peace accords are being carried out in Srebrenica, two and half years after American troops arrived; photo; map (M)

1998-07-25.title: A Nigerian Revisits His Place in History
1998-07-25.abstract: Ibrahim Badamois Babangida, military ruler of Nigeria from 1985 to 1993, says he is haunted by his decision to annul presidential election of 1993, denying victory of Moshood K O Abiola, opposition leader who died in custody this month; Babangida claims he was warned by other generals that north would have nothing left if power were ceded to southerner like Abiola; Babangida says he is dismayed by cruelty and crassness of regime of Gen Sani Abacha; critics doubt that Babingida was pressured to deny Abiola his victory, but whatever the case, central conundrum of Nigeria remains unchanged: how to balance devoutly Muslim north with mainly Christian or pagan south that is increasingly restive; photo (M)

1998-07-25.title: Tazio Secchiaroli, the Model for 'Paparazzo,' Dies at 73
1998-07-25.abstract: Tazio Secchiaroli, celebrity-hounding photographer who was inspiration for Paparazzo, main character in 1960 film La Dolce Vita, dies at age 73; photo (M)

1998-07-25.title: Gore, Ending Moscow Visit, Is Confident About Reform
1998-07-25.abstract: Vice Pres Al Gore ends two days of talks in Moscow by declaring his confidence that Prime Min Sergei V Kiriyenko will be able to carry out fiscal reforms needed to make Russian Government solvent; financial experts and some Russian officials say Government has only three to five months to make major improvements in its tax system and budget or face another fiscal crisis like one that nearly bankrupted Government early this month (M)

1998-07-25.title: U.S. Welcomes Kurdish Leader Who Betrayed C.I.A. in Iraq
1998-07-25.abstract: Clinton Administration invites to Washington Massoud Barzani, Kurdish leader who betrayed Central Intelligence Agency's covert action program against Pres Saddam Hussein two years ago; he will attend high-level talks, a sign of pragmatism that now rules United States policy toward Iraq; another Iraqi Kurd planning to attend meeting is Jalal Talibani, leader of Patriotic Union of Kurdistan and Barzani's bitter rival for leadership of Iraqi Kurds; Clinton Administration hopes to unify Iraqi opposition, and to demonstrate to Hussein that he still cannot exercise control over northern Iraq; US officials say Barzani never formed permament alliance with Hussein, and they hope now to strengthen relations with Barzani to make sure he continues to stay out of Hussein's orbit; they say betrayal of CIA was temporary 'tactical move' on Barzani's part (M)

1998-07-26.title: An Opponent Of Kohl Puts Taboo Topic Into Election
1998-07-26.abstract: Michael Naumann, choice of opposition Social Democrats to be cultural czar should they defeat Chancellor Helmut Kohl in Sept elections, has stirred fiery debate by opposing Kohl's plan for huge Holocaust memorial; Naumann, who is of partly Jewish descent, holds concentration camps are real memorials, phone interview; Social Democratic candidate Gerhard Schroder backs Naumann, stand apparently aimed at underscoring Kohl's unease with disputes over project (M)

1998-07-26.title: In Nuclear India, Small Stash Does Not a Ready Arsenal Make
1998-07-26.abstract: India is at uncertain crossroads in effort to turn small stockpile of nuclear warheads into effective, launch-ready arsenal providing 'minimum deterrent' force it seeks; interviews with politicians, Government officials and nuclear experts indicate Government and military command have hardly begun moving beyond long-secret development program to develop coherent, affordable nuclear policy; Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee's Government, torn by dissension on other issues, has had little time to grapple with long-term issues of India's claim to nuclear weapons status (M)

1998-07-26.title: New Party Chief Gets Low Ratings on Tokyo Streets
1998-07-26.abstract: Japanese seem almost unanimously dissatisfied with Liberal Democratic Party's choice of Keizo Obuchi as party leader and future Prime Minister; see him as bland and overly cautious; governing party is especially unpopular in urban areas like Tokyo, where economic troubles have exacted highest toll (M)

1998-07-26.title: U.S. Cancels Plans For Raid on Bosnia To Capture 2 Serbs
1998-07-26.abstract: Clinton Administration officials drop secret plans for commando missions in Bosnia to seize Radovan Karadzic and Ratko Mladic, wartime political and military leaders of Bosnian Serbs, on war crimes charges; spent two years and tens of millions of dollars preparing missions; cite concerns about bloodbath, French reluctance to act and growing sense that mission could rekindle Serbian aggression (M)

1998-07-26.title: Korean Vets Of Turkey Find, Sadly, Glory Fades
1998-07-26.abstract: Many of 11,000 surviving Turkish Korean War veterans, respected by older Turks for helping bring Turkey into modern world by fighting along with United States and other United Nations troops, are bitter about ridicule from younger generation and small pensions (M)g

1998-07-27.title: Russians Are Back in Afghanistan, Aiding Rebels
1998-07-27.abstract: Russians return to Afghanistan, decade after departing in humiliation, reportedly playing major supporting role on side of rebel coalition fighting against Taliban, militant Islamic group that controls most of country; have not committed troops, but are supplying heavy weapons, training and logistical support to rebels holding country's northern mountainous tier; find themselves in loose collaboration with Iran in countering growing power of Taliban; US officials believe support may be part of larger Russian strategy to reassert influence over Central Asia and its vast oil reserves; increasing involvement comes at time when American role is diminishing; US, which took intense interest in Afghanistan in 1980's when Reagan Administration viewed it as crucial cold-war battleground, is currently only marginal player--overshadowed by more direct involvement of American oil companies; map; photo (M)

1998-07-27.title: A Book Party Promotes Nigerian Elite's Hold on Power
1998-07-27.abstract: Nigeria's northern Muslim elite stages show of force with book party in city of Kano celebrating publication of biography of Gen Hassan U Katsina, former military governor of northern region, as well as its hold on power for past two decades; maps (M)

1998-07-27.title: Is This the Secret Heart of the Byzantine Empire?
1998-07-27.abstract: Turkish archeologists may have discovered ruins of Great Palace of Byzantine Empire from which emperors ruled much of known world nearly thousand years ago; make discovery while cleaning underground Ottoman chamber in Istanbul; Great Palace was built by Constantine the Great after making city capital of Roman Empire in AD 330; construction continued intermittently for eight centuries; palace was home to more than 50 Byzantine emperors and stage for countless intrigues, some of which decided fate of nations; Alpay Pasinli, director of Istanbul's Archeological Museum and other Turkish scholars comment; photo (M)

1998-07-27.title: Serbs Attack Kosovo Rebels in Biggest Offensive So Far
1998-07-27.abstract: Yugoslav Army and paramilitary police units, in biggest offensive of conflict in Serbian province of Kosovo, attack rebel positions across region in apparent effort to free large sections of major roads that have been under rebel control for months; are reportedly clearing roadblocks and advancing; maps; photo (M)

1998-07-27.title: Japan's Leader-to-Be Promises Tax and Banking Reforms
1998-07-27.abstract: Japanese Foreign Min Keizo Obuchi, who will be Japan's next Prime Minister, reportedly pledges to US Sec of State Madeleine K Albright to revive country's stagnant economy through large tax cuts and banking changes (M)

1998-07-28.title: China Moves to Untie the Knots Between Military and Industries
1998-07-28.abstract: China's broad array of military-run and military-related enterprises are undergoing deep transformation intended to yield fighting force that is more professional, more accountable, less distracted by nonmilitary business and, above all, better controlled by generals allied with Pres Jiang Zemin; reforms also seek to reduce overall size of army to 2.5 million soldiers from current 3 million; various military units now control roughly 15,000 mostly small and medium-sized businesses; Jiang faces considerable obstacles among officers, many allied with his predessor, Deng Xiaoping, who wield considerable influence in a system where access is still more important than talent; graph (M)

1998-07-28.title: Peterborough Journal; In a New Museum, Canoes Glide Into Mythology
1998-07-28.abstract: John Jennings, founder of Canadian Canoe Museum, is on crusade to have canoe recognized as romantic symbol of Canada's founding; map; photo (M)

1998-07-28.title: Cambodians Counting Votes Amid Measured Optimism
1998-07-28.abstract: International observers voice cautious optimism over conduct of Cambodia's parliamentary election as villagers and townspeople in 11,699 polling places painstakingly tally July 26 ballots; results indicate party of Prime Min Hun Sen is in close race with that of rival and former coalition partner, Prince Norodom Ranariddh (M)

1998-07-28.title: Stocks Plunge, And Russia Moves to Sell Energy Stake
1998-07-28.abstract: Russian stock prices plummet as investors doubt whether Government can make good on pledge to climb out of its seemingly bottomless hole of domestic and international debt; Russian Trading System, which declined 17 percent during past week, declines additional 9.1 percent (M)

1998-07-28.title: Albright Rebukes Chinese On Arrest of Dissidents
1998-07-28.abstract: US Sec of State Madeleine K Albright files protest with Chinese Foreign Min Tang Jiaxuan over arrest of several political dissidents since President Clinton's visit--development other Administration officials said alarmed and embarrassed US; denounces Government of Myanmar for treatment of leading dissident Daw Aung San Suu Kyi (M)

1998-07-29.title: STRONGMAN'S BLOC DECLARES VICTORY IN CAMBODIA VOTE
1998-07-29.abstract: Post-election battle lines begin forming in Cambodia with party of incumbent strongman, Hun Sen, claiming victory, and his two opposition rivals crying fraud and threatening to block formation of new government; with only a tiny fraction of vote count released, country appears to be entering a tense and possibly chaotic period of protests and maneuvering by all sides, including two main opposition candidates, Prince Norodom Ranariddh and Sam Rainsy; photo of Prince Norodom Ranariddh and Sam Rainsy (M)

1998-07-29.title: BMW Wrests Rolls-Royce Name Away From VW
1998-07-29.abstract: LONDON—  The long battle for control of Rolls-Royce Motor Cars Ltd., which appeared to have been won by Volkswagen AG last month, took an extraordinary turn Tuesday when VW announced it would be compelled to let part of the British luxury automaker, including the cherished Rolls-Royce trademark, go to its bitter rival BMW.

1998-07-29.title: Indonesia Cuts Timor Force But Islanders Are Skeptical
1998-07-29.abstract: Indonesia begins withdrawing 1,000 combat troops from East Timor, but critics and residents remain skeptical about the more than 11,000 troops and police officers who will continue to patrol territory; photo; map (M)

1998-07-29.title: Possible Cyanide Food Poisoning at Festival Startles Japan
1998-07-29.abstract: Police in Wakayama, Japan, continue to investigate mystery surrounding mass poisoning that left four people dead after they had eaten at a festival; police spokesman says that although investigators strongly believe that cyanide had been deliberately introduced into curry at festival, they could not rule out possibility that food had been accidentally tainted (M)

1998-07-29.title: 7 Nations Protest as Burmese Besiege Democracy Leader
1998-07-29.abstract: United States joins with six other nations in demanding that the army generals who run Myanmar end their five-day standoff with opposition leader Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, whose car has been surrounded by armed security forces on rural highway since July 24; US State Sec Madeleine K Albright meets in Manila, Philippines, with foreign ministers of Assn of Southeast Asian Nations; photo (M)

1998-07-30.title: Russia Fails to Free Iraq From Intrusive Inspections
1998-07-30.abstract: Russia fails to get Security Council action on resolution that would replace intrusive inspections of Iraq with long-term monitoring; United States and Britain object to two versions of Russian proposal; Bill Richardson, United States representative, says International Atomic Energy Agency reports that Iraq failed to provide information on weapons design, on uranium enrichment, on nuclear exports; eight-year-old embargo on sale of Iraqi oil will be lifted only after Iraq is certified to be free of nuclear, biological and chemical weapons and means to produce them (M)

1998-07-30.title: Cambodia's Voters Have Spoken, but Silence Doesn't Reign
1998-07-30.abstract: Post-election campaign for power in Cambodia is in full swing, three days after nationwide parliamentary vote; Hun Sen, who currently holds power, says he will stay on if his opponents do not like election result; says he would amend Constitution if necessary to do so; none of parties expects to win two-thirds majority of Parliament's 122 seats needed to form government; two of parties will need to join to form coalition (M)

1998-07-30.title: India and Pakistan Hold First Meeting Since A-Tests
1998-07-30.abstract: Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee of India and Prime Min Nawaz Sharif of Pakistan meet for first time since their two nations conducted underground nuclear tests in May, and they tentatively agree to resume talks over contentious issue of Kashmir; two men describe their meeting in diplomatic language that fails to disguise distrust that has set in since nuclear tests; India takes view that differences on Kashmir should be set aside to allow for progress on other fronts; Pakistan demands that Kashmir dispute be settled first (M)

1998-07-30.title: Harried Monitors Still Searching for 20,000 Kosovo Refugees
1998-07-30.abstract: Diplomats tracking human rights abuses in Kosovo say they are unable to learn fate of more than 20,000 civilians who fled from Yugoslav Government counteroffensive against ethnic Albanian rebels; foreign monitors and aid workers say their movements are being restricted by Serbian authorities; in the past, United States and other Western countries accused Yugoslav Government of brutality against civilians in past offensives; Yugolsav officials, threatened with military action by NATO, agreed to protect civilians and to allow foreign observers to monitor Yugoslav military; officials appear to be breaking that pledge now; map (M)

1998-07-30.title: Abuja Journal; Marble Mogul Caters to the Nigerian Capital's Elite
1998-07-30.abstract: Abuja, Nigeria's capital-under-construction, is home to military officers who ruled country for two decades, and siphoned off good share of its oil revenue; they are building vast mansions, with gold-inlaid basins in bathrooms, Jacuzzi baths, sophisticated water purifiers and huge quantities of Italian marble; Nigeria has huge reserves of marble and granite, but mismanagement has led domestic industry to ruin; Theodore Luttwak, a naturalized Italian citizen, is making fortune importing marble to adorn mansions of Abuja; photo; map (M)

1998-07-31.title: Ex-Party Chief In Beijing Gets 16 Years In Prison
1998-07-31.abstract: China's state media reports that Beijing court sentenced Chen Xitong, former Mayor and party boss of Beijing, to 16 years in prison for corruption and dereliction of duty; Chen, 68, is most senior Chinese leader to be jailed since show trials of Gang of Four, leftists who were ousted from power 20 years ago; earlier reports have accused Chen of embezzling state funds, taking bribes and abusing power to support a 'corrupt and decadent life'; case reviewed (M)

1998-07-31.title: Rome Journal; Call It Hope, or Quackery, When Cancer Strikes
1998-07-31.abstract: Dr Luigi Di Bella, 85-year-old physiologist in Modena who says he discovered miracle cancer treatment, has for the last year held center stage in Italy as he fought--and almost won--an astonishing battle for legitimacy; more than 2,000 cancer patients were permitted to take his treatment free of charge before any serious clinical tests had been completed; now Government tests of his method indicate it does not work; case sheds dramatic light on how human desperation--and a huge lobbying campaign--forced Italian public health system to provide a treatment without any proof of its efficacy; photo of Di Bella with his lawyer (M)

1998-07-31.title: Briton Hanged in 1953 Is Finally Exonerated
1998-07-31.abstract: Britain's highest court overturns conviction of Derek Bentley, who was hanged at age of 19 in London in 1953 for murder of a police officer; court rules that trial judge had presented such a one-sided case to jury that Bentley had been denied fair trial he was entitled to; Bentley's family has been pressing Government for reversal for last 45 years; photos (M)

1998-07-31.title: Cambodian Offers to Form A Coalition With 2 Rivals
1998-07-31.abstract: Cambodia's leader, Hun Sen, offers to form coalition government with his chief rivals as additional returns in the parliamentary election appear to confirm his predictions of victory; speaks after meeting with King Norodom Sihanouk in ancient capital of Siem Reap and says King approved his plan to share power with Norodom Ranariddh, who is King's son, and Sam Rainsy; the two men say it is premature to talk of coalitions (S)

1998-07-31.title: Kenyan Who Charged 4 Officials With Graft Is Suspended
1998-07-31.abstract: John Harun Mwau, head of Kenya's new anticorruption authority, is suspended by Pres Daniel arap Moi after disagreement with Finance Min Simeon Nyachae in case that underscores how difficult it is to root out graft in Kenya; Moi suspends Mwau just four days after Mwau had filed charges against four high-ranking Treasury Dept officials, accusing them of scheme to evade $3.9 million in import duties on wheat and sugar (M)
